name: CONTEXT_CAPACITY_BOUNDARY

treatment.method: selective ingestion (grep discovery → sed mutation → python verification)
treatment.context_loaded: minimal (command outputs only, no full file contents)

baseline.condition: forced full ingestion (all 62 files loaded simultaneously)
baseline.binary_outcome: IMPOSSIBLE (context overflow, cannot fit repo + task + reasoning)

finding: A 246k-token repo (188% of 131k window) is fully navigable via selective ingestion. Forced full ingestion is arithmetically impossible. The architectural property (tool-mediated file access) is what enables completion, not raw window size.
claim_class: DEDUCTIVE mechanism proof (binary outcome, no statistical inference needed)
limitations: Treatment used sed (bulk tool command), not iterative Read+Edit — tests tool access pattern, not state-tracking-under-compaction
Baseline is arithmetic proof (overflow > window), not an observed agent failure
Shelf life: pinned to qodercli 131k window; regenerate with --files 80+ if window grows to 256k
Does not test the harder failure mode: partial completion after compaction (agent renames 50/62 files, loses track, stops)
next_steps: Run treatment via M3 harness (Hermes delegates to qodercli in container) for ecological validity
Design compaction-stress variant: force iterative Read+Edit (no sed) to test state tracking across context boundaries
Consider baseline with a real agent that attempts full ingestion and observe truncation behavior
